q: The smallest unit of matter that can exist independently is called a/an:
sol: A molecule is the smallest particle of an element or compound that can exist independently.

q: Which state of matter has a definite shape and a definite volume?
sol: Solids have a rigid structure, giving them a fixed shape and volume.

q: The space between the particles of matter is known as:
sol: The gaps between molecules are called intermolecular spaces.

q: Which state of matter has the maximum intermolecular force of attraction?
sol: In solids, particles are very close and strongly attracted to each other.

q: The process of a solid changing directly into a gas is called:
sol: Sublimation is the direct transition from solid to gas without becoming a liquid.

q: Matter is made up of very tiny particles called:
sol: Matter consists of minute particles known as atoms or molecules.

q: Which of the following describes the movement of particles in a gas?
sol: Gas particles have high kinetic energy and move freely in all directions.

q: The force of attraction between similar molecules is called:
sol: Cohesion is the force of attraction between molecules of the same substance.

q: Kinetic energy of molecules ________ with an increase in temperature.
sol: Heating provides energy to molecules, causing them to move faster.

q: Which of these is highly compressible?
sol: Gases have large intermolecular spaces, allowing them to be easily compressed.

q: The temperature at which a liquid changes into a solid is its:
sol: Freezing point is the temperature of transition from liquid to solid.

q: Molecules in a liquid:
sol: Liquid molecules have enough energy to move around but stay within the bulk.

q: The intermolecular space is minimum in:
sol: Solids are closely packed, leaving very little space between particles.

q: Which of the following is NOT matter?
sol: Light is energy, not matter, as it lacks mass and does not occupy space.

q: Brownian motion refers to:
sol: Brownian motion is the random, erratic movement of microscopic particles.

q: The force of attraction between different types of molecules is called:
sol: Adhesion is the attraction between molecules of different substances.

q: What happens to the intermolecular space when a liquid evaporates?
sol: Molecules move further apart as they turn into gas.

q: Which of these substances undergoes sublimation?
sol: Camphor turns directly into vapor upon heating.

q: Fluids include:
sol: Any substance that can flow (liquids and gases) is a fluid.

q: Diffusion is fastest in:
sol: High speed and large spaces in gases allow for rapid mixing.

q: The boiling point of water is:
sol: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.

q: During melting, the temperature of a substance:
sol: The temperature stays the same until the entire solid has melted (latent heat).

q: Which of the following has a fixed volume but no fixed shape?
sol: Milk (a liquid) takes the shape of its container but maintains its volume.

q: Molecules of a gas exert ________ on the walls of the container.
sol: Collisions of gas molecules against walls result in pressure.

q: Change of state from gas to liquid is called:
sol: Condensation is the process of cooling gas into liquid.

q: Which is an example of a molecule of an element?
sol: O2 consists of only one type of atom (Oxygen).

q: Solids generally have ________ density.
sol: Close packing of molecules makes solids dense.

q: The intermolecular force in gases is:
sol: Gases have very weak attractive forces between particles.

q: Liquids have ________ rates of diffusion compared to solids.
sol: Particles move more freely in liquids than in solids.

q: Dry ice is:
sol: Solid CO2 is called dry ice because it sublimes without melting.

q: Atoms combine to form:
sol: Molecules are groups of two or more atoms held together.

q: Which of the following is most easily compressed?
sol: Air is a gas and has high compressibility.

q: The kinetic energy of molecules is highest in:
sol: Steam (gas) has higher energy than liquid or solid at the same or lower temps.

q: Liquids can flow because:
o: Molecules can move past each other
sol: Weaker intermolecular forces in liquids allow particles to glide.

q: Conversion of liquid into vapor below its boiling point is:
sol: Evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature below boiling.

q: Which state of matter has no definite shape and no definite volume?
sol: Gases expand to fill any container shape and size.

q: The symbol 'H' represents an:
sol: Single letters usually represent individual atoms of an element.

q: When a solid melts, its molecules:
sol: Absorbed heat increases the energy of the particles.

q: The smell of perfume spreads in a room due to:
sol: Diffusion is the movement of particles from high to low concentration.

q: Intermolecular force is also known as:
sol: These are the forces holding molecules together.

q: Which of the following is a characteristic of solids?
sol: High molecular packing results in high density.

q: Gas molecules move in ________ path until they collide.
sol: Molecules travel in straight lines between collisions.

q: Which state has the least kinetic energy?
sol: Solid particles only vibrate in fixed positions.

q: Water exists in ________ states.
sol: Water exists as ice (solid), water (liquid), and steam (gas).

q: An atom is ________.
sol: All matter is built from atoms.

q: The movement of molecules in solids is ________.
sol: Solids vibrate about a mean position.

q: Which of these will occupy the entire space of a container?
sol: Gases like Oxygen expand to fill the entire available volume.

q: The melting point of ice is:
sol: Ice melts at 0 degrees Celsius.

q: Which of the following describes a liquid?
sol: Liquids have a constant volume but take the container's shape.

q: Force of attraction is ________ in gases.
sol: Gases have the weakest attractive forces among common states.

q: Matter occupies ________ and has ________.
sol: Definition of matter: anything with mass and volume.

q: Naphthalene balls disappear after some time due to:
sol: Naphthalene sublimes directly into gas.

q: A pure substance made of only one kind of atom is an:
sol: Elements consist of identical atoms.

q: The chemical formula for water is:
sol: Water consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

q: Molecules of a liquid move ________ than molecules of a solid.
sol: Liquids have higher kinetic energy than solids.

q: Surface tension is a property of:
sol: Surface tension arises from cohesive forces in liquids.

q: Which state of matter is rigid?
sol: Solids maintain their shape due to rigidity.

q: Solubility of a solid in a liquid generally ________ with temperature.
sol: Higher temperature usually allows more solid to dissolve.

q: Everything in the universe is made of:
sol: Matter and energy make up the universe.

q: Gases can be ________ easily.
sol: Compression is a key property of gases.

q: A compound is made of ________ elements.
sol: Compounds consist of different atoms chemically bonded.

q: Molecules are always in ________ motion.
sol: Particles of matter never stop moving.

q: Density is calculated as ________.
sol: Density = Mass per unit Volume.

q: The process of a gas changing to a solid is:
sol: Deposition is the reverse of sublimation.

q: Solids do not ________.
sol: Due to strong forces, solid particles do not mix/diffuse easily.

q: Intermolecular space in liquids is ________ than in solids.
sol: Liquids are less tightly packed than solids.

q: Rate of evaporation increases with ________.
sol: More surface area allows more molecules to escape.

q: Air is a ________.
sol: Air contains various gases like N2, O2, CO2 mixed physically.

q: Which of these is a monoatomic molecule?
sol: Noble gases like Helium exist as single atoms.

q: Which state has the most space between molecules?
sol: Gases have the largest intermolecular distances.

q: Iodine crystals on heating show ________.
sol: Iodine is a classic example of a sublimable solid.

q: Atomicity of Oxygen (O2) is ________.
sol: Oxygen gas contains two atoms per molecule.

q: Solidification is also called ________.
sol: Turning liquid to solid is freezing.

q: Which factor does NOT affect the state of matter?
sol: Color is an optical property, not a physical driver of state.

q: A gas has ________ boundary.
sol: Gases spread out and have no surfaces.

q: Diffusion occurs from ________ concentration to ________ concentration.
sol: Particles spread out from where they are crowded.

q: In which state is the movement of particles most restricted?
sol: Strong forces keep solid particles in fixed spots.

q: Which of the following is an example of adhesion?
sol: Attraction between water and glass (different materials) is adhesion.

q: Which state of matter can expand the most?
sol: Gases expand to fill any volume available.

q: The amount of matter in an object is its ________.
sol: Mass is the measure of the quantity of matter.

q: Force of attraction is ________ in solids.
sol: Solids have the strongest cohesive forces.

q: Which state of matter has the highest density?
sol: Generally, solids are the densest state for a substance.

q: Boiling is a ________ phenomenon.
sol: Boiling involves the whole mass of the liquid.

q: Molecules of a gas are ________.
sol: High speed and large distances characterize gas particles.

q: Substances like sponge are solids but can be compressed because of ________.
sol: The air inside the holes is what gets compressed.

q: Liquids have a ________ shape.
sol: Liquids flow to match the container.

q: On cooling, the kinetic energy of molecules ________.
sol: Removing heat slows down the particles.

q: The symbol for Gold is ________.
sol: Au comes from the Latin word 'Aurum'.

q: One example of a diatomic gas is ________.
sol: Nitrogen exists as pairs of atoms.

q: Particles of matter have ________ between them.
sol: There are intermolecular spaces in all matter.

q: The change from liquid to gas at a fixed temperature is ________.
sol: Boiling occurs at a specific temperature (Boiling Point).

q: A molecule made of three atoms of the same element is ________.
sol: Ozone is triatomic.

q: Liquids exert pressure in ________ directions.
sol: Fluids exert pressure uniformly in all directions.

q: Solids have ________ compressibility.
sol: Solids are almost impossible to compress further.

q: Intermolecular force ________ as distance increases.
sol: Attractive forces weaken when particles move apart.

q: Which is a chemical change?
sol: Burning creates new substances, unlike physical changes.

q: Molecules of a solid ________.
sol: Particles are fixed but vibrate.

q: Kinetic theory explains the ________ of matter.
sol: It describes how particle motion determines physical state.

q: Matter is made of ________.
sol: Matter has a particulate nature.

q: Evaporation causes ________.
sol: Escaping high-energy molecules leave the rest cooler.
